hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — Jack, 8, Jack. You bet. Your opponent called.
Turn — 10. You checked. Your opponent bet — bigger than expected.

wrongReasons: 5 K has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with 5 K.
7 9 makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
K 4 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only one pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: Q 10

Your opponent called the flop with one pair, then picked up two pair on the turn. The sudden aggression on the turn — after passivity on the flop — is the tell: they improved and immediately started building the pot.
